steps.title: Identify the perfect squares
steps.content:
Examine each term in $256w^2 - 676$.
$256w^2 = (16w)^2$ because $16^2 = 256$.
$676 = 26^2$ because $26^2 = 676$.
So the expression is $(16w)^2 - 26^2$, which is a difference of squares.

steps.title: Apply the difference of squares formula
steps.content:
With $a = 16w$ and $b = 26$, we get: $(16w - 26)(16w + 26)$.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
